title: Find the Minimum Number of Fibonacci Numbers Whose Sum Is K
hints: Notice that Fibonacci numbers grow exponentially, so there are very few distinct values below 10^9. How does this affect your search strategy?
Try a greedy approach: always subtract the largest possible Fibonacci number from k. Does this strategy always yield the minimum count?
Zeckendorf's theorem states every integer has a unique sum of non-consecutive Fibonacci numbers. Does allowing reuse change the optimal strategy compared to this unique representation?
